definition: Tencent messaging and social platform
examples: WeChat is widely used in China.

definition: Mobile payment service inside WeChat
examples: WeChat Pay supports QR-code payments.

definition: Open-access repository for scholarly preprints
examples: The paper first appeared on arXiv.

definition: Search engine for biomedical literature
examples: We searched PubMed for randomized trials.

definition: Digital Object Identifier for a publication
examples: The DOI links to the article record.

definition: Identifier assigned to a PubMed record

definition: Persistent identifier for researchers
examples: The author provided an ORCID iD.

definition: Abbreviation meaning and others
examples: Smith et al. reported similar results.

definition: Performed outside a living organism
examples: The assay was performed in vitro.

definition: Performed inside a living organism
examples: The effect was validated in vivo.

definition: Probability measure used in statistical hypothesis testing
examples: A p-value below 0.05 was considered significant.

definition: Range of values likely to contain an estimated parameter
examples: The confidence interval was narrow.

definition: Quantitative measure of the magnitude of an effect
examples: The study reported a small effect size.

definition: Likelihood that an observed result is not due to chance
examples: The result reached statistical significance.

definition: Study design that randomly assigns participants to groups
examples: The randomized controlled trial tested the intervention.

definition: Structured review of evidence using predefined methods
examples: The systematic review included 42 studies.

definition: Statistical synthesis of results from multiple studies
examples: The meta-analysis found a modest benefit.

definition: Observational study following a group over time
examples: The cohort study tracked patients for five years.

definition: Study observing variables at one point in time
examples: The cross-sectional study measured prevalence.

definition: Study comparing individuals with and without an outcome
examples: The case-control study examined risk factors.

definition: Machine-learning model trained on large text corpora
examples: Large language models can generate fluent text.

definition: Abbreviation for large language model
examples: The LLM was evaluated on reasoning tasks.

definition: Neural network architecture based on attention
examples: The Transformer architecture improved sequence modeling.

definition: Neural network mechanism that weights relevant information
examples: The attention mechanism highlights important tokens.

definition: Attention mechanism within the same sequence
examples: Self-attention connects tokens in a sentence.

definition: Vector representation of data such as words or documents
examples: The embedding captures semantic similarity.

definition: Adapting a pretrained model to a specific task
examples: Fine-tuning improved domain performance.

definition: Designing prompts to guide model behavior
examples: Prompt engineering changed the model output.

definition: Generation method that retrieves external knowledge first
examples: Retrieval-augmented generation reduces hallucination.

definition: Abbreviation for retrieval-augmented generation
examples: RAG adds retrieved passages to the prompt.

definition: Algorithm for computing gradients in neural networks
examples: Backpropagation updates the model parameters.

definition: Optimization algorithm that follows negative gradients
examples: Gradient descent minimized the loss function.

definition: Model performs well on training data but poorly on new data
examples: Regularization helps reduce overfitting.

definition: Model is too simple to capture the underlying pattern
examples: The shallow model suffered from underfitting.

definition: Computational model composed of connected layers
examples: The neural network classified the image.

definition: Neural network often used for images
examples: The convolutional neural network extracted visual features.

definition: Abbreviation for convolutional neural network
examples: A CNN was used for image classification.

definition: Neural network designed for sequential data
examples: The recurrent neural network processed time series.

definition: Abbreviation for recurrent neural network
examples: The RNN modeled sequential dependencies.

definition: Learning by interacting with an environment and rewards
examples: Reinforcement learning optimized the policy.

definition: Learning from labeled examples
examples: Supervised learning requires labeled data.

definition: Learning patterns without labeled outputs
examples: Unsupervised learning discovered clusters.

definition: Learning from a mix of labeled and unlabeled data
examples: Semi-supervised learning reduced labeling cost.

definition: Using a model on a task without task-specific examples
examples: The model achieved zero-shot transfer.

definition: Using a small number of examples
examples: Few-shot prompting improved accuracy.

definition: Experiment removing components to test their contribution
examples: The ablation study confirmed the module mattered.

definition: Reference method or result used for comparison
examples: The new model outperformed the baseline.

definition: Standard test used to compare systems
examples: The benchmark measures translation quality.

definition: Best known performance or method at a given time
examples: The method achieved state-of-the-art accuracy.

definition: Abbreviation for state of the art
examples: The model reached SOTA on the benchmark.

definition: Collection of data used for analysis or training
examples: The dataset contains annotated images.

definition: Technique that expands training data with transformations
examples: Data augmentation improved robustness.

definition: Evaluation method splitting data into multiple folds
examples: Cross-validation produced a stable estimate.

definition: Technique to reduce overfitting by adding constraints

definition: Scaling values to a common range or distribution
examples: Normalization stabilized training.

definition: Dimensionality reduction method using orthogonal components
examples: Principal component analysis revealed clusters.

definition: Abbreviation for principal component analysis
examples: PCA reduced the feature dimension.

definition: Study scanning genetic variants associated with traits

definition: Abbreviation for genome-wide association study

definition: Sequencing method for transcriptome analysis

definition: Single-cell RNA sequencing method

definition: Sequencing technology at single-cell resolution

definition: Complete set of RNA transcripts in a cell or tissue

definition: Genetic constitution of an organism or variant

definition: Observable traits or characteristics

definition: Computational analysis of biological data
examples: Bioinformatics tools processed sequencing reads.

definition: Manuscript shared publicly before peer review
examples: The preprint has not yet been peer reviewed.

definition: Evaluation of scholarly work by experts
examples: Peer review improved the manuscript.

definition: Draft or submitted academic paper
examples: The manuscript was submitted to the journal.

definition: Reference to a source or publication

definition: List of references in a document

definition: Additional files or data accompanying a paper
examples: The supplementary material contains extra tables.
